mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and pushing S&P 500 prices higher within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit expansion and consumer confidence, which jointly amplify equity demand and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in forward 20-day returns across three consecutive calendar years, or partial correlation with SPX drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained growth in industrial production signals peak capacity utilization and impending supply bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% annualized during late-cycle periods, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.2 percent triggers immediate market repricing of imminent Federal Reserve rate hikes, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations within a one-week window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.2% threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance after adjusting for FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands the corporate earnings base, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth remains positive, or the rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in total nonfarm payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which drives consumer spending and lifts S&P 500 equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 45% of observed instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when added to a single-proxy baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment, a rising CPIAUCSL reading compresses real interest rates, forcing equity investors to chase yield and pushing S&P 500 prices higher within a one-week window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
